description: Add an exclusive `UseRelationalBackend` repository swap in the host composition root. Acceptance: when `UseRelationalBackend=false`, DI still registers only the legacy `PostgresqlDocumentStoreRepository`; when `UseRelationalBackend=true`, DI registers only a new `RelationalDocumentStoreRepository` as `IDocumentStoreRepository`, adds the full relational runtime prerequisites for PostgreSQL deployments, and adds the matching full relational runtime prerequisites for MSSQL deployments, including runtime mapping-set compilation or provider services plus the dialect-specific relational command and reference-resolution services required by the new repository. No operation delegates back to the legacy repository when the flag is on.
steps-to-verify: Add frontend/unit composition tests for PostgreSQL with the flag off, PostgreSQL with the flag on, and MSSQL with the flag on.

description: Define the backend-local DMS-983 write contracts actually supported in this branch: relational operation kind, target context, `FlatteningInput`, `FlattenedWriteSet`, root row buffer, root-extension rows, collection candidates, candidate-attached aligned-scope data, and `IRelationalWriteTerminalStage`. Acceptance: contracts use the selected `JsonNode` body rather than Core profile types, root `DocumentId` and new `CollectionItemId` remain unresolved for create flows, and collection-aligned 1:1 scopes such as `$.addresses[*]._ext.sample` remain attached to the owning collection candidate instead of being emitted as standalone bound rows.
steps-to-verify: Add backend/unit contract tests that lock down the unresolved-key and candidate-attached-scope behavior.

description: Implement `RelationalDocumentStoreRepository` as the exclusive opt-in write repository. Acceptance: POST and PUT for non-descriptor resources enter the relational seam; GET and DELETE fail fast with `UnknownFailure` and precise not-implemented messages; descriptor POST and PUT also fail fast with precise not-implemented messages; missing non-descriptor `ResourceWritePlan` and unsupported relational gaps return deterministic `UnknownFailure` messages; no legacy fallback path is used anywhere.
steps-to-verify: Add backend/unit tests for unsupported operations, descriptor writes, and missing-plan guard rails.

description: Add target-context selection ahead of flattening. Acceptance: POST performs real relational upsert existence detection using the request `ReferentialId` and passes `create-new` versus `existing` target context to the terminal stage, including existing `DocumentId` and `DocumentUuid` when found; PUT resolves the requested `DocumentUuid` to existing-document context before flattening and passes an update target context that includes the real existing `DocumentId` and `DocumentUuid` when found, while still deferring legacy ETag, immutable-identity, no-op, and not-found behavior in this story.
steps-to-verify: Add backend/unit tests for POST create-new detection, POST existing-document detection, PUT existing-document context resolution, and the resulting PUT target-context shape.

description: Integrate `IReferenceResolver` into the relational repository flow. Acceptance: POST and PUT call the real resolver with the selected mapping set and request-extracted references, relational writes short-circuit on missing or incompatible references using the existing `UpsertFailureReference` and `UpdateFailureReference` semantics, and neither the flattener nor the terminal stage is called when reference resolution fails.
steps-to-verify: Add backend/unit tests for successful resolution and short-circuit failure behavior on both document and descriptor failures.

description: Adapt `ResolvedReferenceSet` into flattener-local hot-loop lookups for document and descriptor FKs. Acceptance: compiled document-reference bindings can resolve FK values by ordinal path without runtime JSONPath evaluation, nested collection references map to the correct owning candidate or row, and descriptor lookups use the resolved concrete-path data instead of re-reading source JSON.
steps-to-verify: Add backend/unit tests for root, nested, and repeated reference lookups keyed by ordinal path.

description: Implement the initial flattener walker for the root table and the DMS-983-supported extension scopes. Acceptance: traversal reads the selected body once, emits deterministic values in `TableWritePlan.ColumnBindings` order for the root table and present root-extension scopes, treats the selected body as authoritative input, performs strict scalar reading against compiled types, and does not invoke a general-purpose JSONPath engine in the hot loop.
steps-to-verify: Add backend/unit fixture tests for root and non-collection row emission, including a supplied-body-is-authoritative case.

description: Populate non-storage-assigned write bindings during flattening. Acceptance: `KeyUnificationWritePlan` producers populate canonical storage columns and synthetic presence flags where required, `WriteValueSource.Precomputed` values are filled for non-storage-assigned bindings except storage-assigned root and collection identities, and unified-alias columns are never materialized as writable outputs.
steps-to-verify: Add backend/unit tests against the focused stable-key fixture to prove key-unification precomputed values and presence flags are emitted correctly.

description: Implement collection and common-type candidate extraction. Acceptance: the flattener emits deterministic candidates for top-level and nested collection scopes with ordinal path, request sibling order, semantic-identity values in compiled order, scalar/FK/precomputed values, and attached aligned-scope data; duplicate submitted semantic identities under the same stable parent are detected in DMS-983 and fail deterministically before terminal handoff.
steps-to-verify: Add backend/unit tests for top-level collections, nested collections, request sibling order preservation, and duplicate semantic-identity detection.

description: Handle root-scope `_ext` flattening. Acceptance: root-level extension rows are emitted only when extension data exists, root-level extension child collections flatten into collection candidates with preserved request order, and empty or absent `_ext` sites do not create rows.
steps-to-verify: Add backend/unit tests for root `_ext` row emission and root-level extension child collections.

description: Handle `_ext` within collection and common-type scopes, including collection-aligned one-to-one scopes and aligned extension child collections. Acceptance: aligned one-to-one scopes stay attached to the owning base collection candidate until stable `CollectionItemId` binding exists, nested extension child collections preserve sibling order and semantic identity, and root-level versus collection-aligned attachment rules match `extensions.md`.
steps-to-verify: Add backend/unit tests using the collections-with-nested-extension and focused stable-key extension fixtures.

description: Implement `IRelationalWriteTerminalStage` orchestration and a production default stage for DMS-983. Acceptance: after successful plan selection, target-context resolution, reference resolution, and flattening, the repository invokes the terminal stage with operation kind, target context, `ResourceWritePlan`, selected body, `ResolvedReferenceSet`, flattened output, and trace or diagnostic identifiers; the default production stage returns `UnknownFailure` with a precise not-implemented message; tests can substitute a capturing fake without changing repository logic.
steps-to-verify: Add backend/unit tests for terminal-stage invocation, default not-implemented behavior, and capture-stage substitution.

description: Add Core-fronted seam integration coverage for the new relational write path using a capturing terminal stage. Acceptance: tests cover POST happy path, PUT happy path, reference-failure short-circuit, supplied-body-is-authoritative, mapping-set and write-plan guard rails, one complex fixture with nested collections plus `_ext`, and one top-level HTTP smoke with `UseRelationalBackend=true`; PostgreSQL and SQL Server composition are both covered at the registration and seam level, while heavier DB-backed write coverage remains deferred to DMS-984.
steps-to-verify: Add Core-fronted integration tests in Core and one HTTP smoke in the frontend test project.

description: Fail deterministically when a reference-bearing value is present in the selected write body but no matching `ResolvedReferenceSet` entry exists for the compiled binding and ordinal path. Acceptance: document-reference and descriptor-reference bindings distinguish `reference absent from body` from `reference present but missing from the resolved lookup set`; the missing-lookup case fails before terminal-stage handoff with a precise message that identifies the table or binding and path; the supported Core-fronted seam also surfaces that failure before any terminal-stage invocation; the flattener no longer emits `Literal(null)` for a present reference solely because extraction, lookup adaptation, or ordinal-path matching missed it.
steps-to-verify: Add backend/unit tests where the selected body contains a root document reference, a nested collection document reference, and a descriptor value, but the `ResolvedReferenceSet` omits the corresponding concrete path. Assert that flattening or repository orchestration fails deterministically instead of producing a nullable FK binding.
Add a Core seam test proving the supported relational write entrypoint fails before terminal-stage handoff when a present reference is missing from the resolved lookup set.

description: Narrow and simplify the DMS-983 standalone-scope contract so it matches the compiler shapes that can actually exist today. Acceptance: the branch no longer advertises generic base-resource non-collection row support when the current compiler cannot emit it; `StandaloneScopeWriteRowBuffer`, `NonCollectionRows`, related comments, and the DMS-983 design story are explicitly root-extension-scoped; stale shared-model comments that still imply broader support are corrected as part of the same cleanup; no dead or misleading contract surface remains that implies support for unsupported separate-table 1:1 base scopes.
steps-to-verify: Update backend contract tests and flattener tests to lock down the final supported scope inventory and reject or omit impossible shapes consistently.
Update shared model comments such as `DbTableKind.Collection` documentation so they no longer imply unsupported standalone-scope behavior in this branch.
Update the DMS-983 story text so its contract language matches the runtime contract checked into code.

description: Move `MappingSet` carriage back out of the public Core/backend repository request boundary and keep it backend-local. Acceptance: `EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External` no longer references `EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External`; public `IUpsertRequest` and `IUpdateRequest` do not expose `MappingSet`; the relational path gets the resolved mapping set from an internal request type, internal adapter, or backend-local context instead of expanding the public request contract; legacy repository implementations and external consumers of `Core.External` do not need to take a backend-specific dependency to compile.
steps-to-verify: Refactor the relational repository seam so only internal Core or backend-local request types carry the mapping-set dependency, then remove the `Core.External -> Backend.External` project reference.

description: Clarify and align the missing-`MappingSet` contract across middleware, handlers, repository tests, and comments. Acceptance: missing `MappingSet` remains middleware-owned for supported execution paths and surfaces as a deterministic 503 before repository execution; middleware, handler, and repository-seam comments no longer claim null-tolerant downstream behavior if that is not actually supported; seam tests prove the supported relational path does not rely on an uncaught `ArgumentNullException`; the repository may keep null-argument checks as defensive invariants for unsupported direct-call scenarios, but no supported execution path ends in an uncaught `ArgumentNullException` because `MappingSet` was absent.
steps-to-verify: Add or update unit tests and comments so the middleware-owned contract is explicit and the supported execution path does not rely on an uncaught null-argument exception.
